items.headline: History in Indiana — Caitlin Clark posts the WNBA's first-ever 40-point, 10-assist game
items.body: Clark went supernova Friday night, dropping a career-high 45 points with 10 assists as the Fever edged the Storm 110-107 — the first 40/10 game in league history. She also became the fastest player ever to 200 career threes (74 games), and she and Kelsey Mitchell became the first WNBA teammates to go for 40 and 30 in the same night. The league just named her AP Player of the Week, and the All-Star Game is Saturday.
items.say: Did you see Caitlin Clark went for 45 and 10 the other night? First 40-point, 10-assist game in WNBA history — nobody had ever done it. She's kind of rewriting the record book. You watch any WNBA?

links.text: Caitlin Clark — 45 PTS, first 40/10 game in WNBA history (Indiana Fever)

items.headline: Evenepoel wins the time trial — but Pogačar's still cruising in yellow
items.body: The Tour's only solo time trial delivered a heavyweight clash. World TT champion Remco Evenepoel produced a flawless ride over the tricky 26km from Évian, beating overall leader Tadej Pogačar by 28 seconds on the day with Isaac del Toro third. It closes Evenepoel's gap a touch, but Pogačar keeps a commanding lead as the race heads into its final week — and defending champ Jonas Vingegaard is already out after his crash.
items.say: Evenepoel won the time trial at the Tour today — actually beat Pogačar for once. But Pogačar's still way out front in yellow. Last week of the Tour now. Good background watching this week.

links.text: Cyclingnews · Stage 16 time trial as it happened

items.headline: Silly season, baseball edition — the trade deadline is two weeks out and everyone's a buyer
items.body: The Aug. 3 deadline is looming and it's shaping up wild: with 23 of 30 teams either in a playoff spot or within five games of one at the break, almost nobody wants to sell. The dominoes have started — the Dodgers already grabbed reliever Seth Halvorsen — and names like the Angels' starters are floating around. Expect a frantic couple of weeks of rumors before the buzzer.
items.say: The baseball trade deadline's in a couple weeks and basically every team thinks it's still in it, so it could get nuts. The Dodgers already made a move. You following the deadline stuff at all?

links.text: ESPN · Passan's top deadline moves for contenders

items.headline: Football's basically back — NFL training camps open this week
items.body: The offseason is officially ending: veterans start reporting to camp across the league this week, with the Cardinals and Panthers (who open the Hall of Fame Game on Aug. 6) first up Wednesday and everyone else in by July 28. The defending champion Seahawks are already rolling. Cue the position battles, the 'best shape of his life' stories, and real football on the horizon.
items.say: NFL camps open this week — feels like football's finally almost back. Everybody's got their 'this is our year' optimism right now. You following your team's camp?

links.text: NFL.com · 2026 training camp report dates for all 32 teams

items.headline: CS2 is back on the clock — the BLAST Bounty Summer qualifier fires up today
items.body: Counter-Strike doesn't take the summer off. The BLAST Bounty Summer 2026 online qualifier runs July 21–26, a 32-team single-elimination gauntlet for spots at the LAN finals in Malta. The field's loaded — Falcons (fresh off the Cologne Major), Vitality, a retooled MOUZ and the new 100 Thieves roster are all in the mix. Great low-stakes-but-spicy CS to have on this week.
items.say: CS is back this week — the BLAST Bounty qualifier just started, Falcons and Vitality and all of them scrapping for the Malta finals. You keeping up with Counter-Strike lately?

links.text: Esports.gg · BLAST Bounty Summer qualifier details & results

items.headline: Charli XCX flips the script — 'Music, Fashion, Film' lands Friday
items.body: The BRAT phenom is back with her seventh album, out July 24, and she's swerving hard: an 11-track, 30-minute set she says 'flips the form' away from the electropop she's known for. The rollout's been very Charli — a cover starring John Cale, Marc Jacobs and Martin Scorsese, plus singles 'Rock Music' and 'SS26' and a brand-new video for 'camera.' Another summer, another Charli takeover.
items.say: Charli XCX has a new album Friday — the follow-up to Brat, and apparently it sounds totally different this time. That whole rollout's been wild. You were into Brat, right?

links.text: Variety · 'Music, Fashion, Film' — release date & Scorsese cover

items.headline: 'Motor City' — the Alan Ritchson revenge thriller with almost no dialogue — opens Friday
items.body: One for action fans: 'Reacher' star Alan Ritchson leads this 1970s-Detroit revenge saga as a working-class guy framed and out for payback, and the hook is it's told almost entirely without dialogue — the story rides on action and a killer soundtrack curated by Jack White. Shailene Woodley, Ben Foster and Pablo Schreiber co-star. It hits theaters July 24 and the trailer alone is a whole mood.
items.say: There's a new Alan Ritchson movie Friday, Motor City — it's a revenge thing set in '70s Detroit with basically no dialogue, just action and music. The trailer's awesome. You a Reacher guy?

links.text: MOTOR CITY — Official Trailer (Independent Film Company)
FirstShowing · Alan Ritchson's gritty revenge film 'Motor City'

items.headline: 'WWE: Unreal' is back — the wrestling docuseries drops Season 3 today
items.body: Netflix's peek inside the WWE writers' room returns today with five new episodes, and this season's a big one: it follows the road to WrestleMania 42 as John Cena says goodbye, AJ Lee returns, and a Seth Rollins injury forces last-minute rewrites. Even if you don't watch wrestling, it's a fascinating look at how the sausage gets made — the 'Drive to Survive' of sports entertainment.
items.say: New season of WWE: Unreal just dropped on Netflix — it goes behind the scenes in the writers' room, Cena's whole goodbye. Kind of the Drive to Survive of wrestling. Would you watch that?

links.text: Netflix Tudum · 'WWE: Unreal' Season 3 cast & release
